req.title: Message to provider
req.statement: A patient shall send a message to their provider; messages containing attachments shall scan for disallowed PHI exfiltration patterns.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers three testable dimensions: (1) happy-path message delivery with no attachment (EP – valid partition), (2) blocked submission when an attachment contains disallowed PHI exfiltration patterns (NEG / DT – blocked branch of the decision table), and (3) a boundary case for an attachment at exactly the maximum allowed size with no PHI, which should still succeed (BVA). A fourth test covers the state transition from 'draft → sent → provider-notified' to verify the full lifecycle (STC). Together these four cases satisfy the minimum three required and give traceability across all expected outcomes and error pathways stated in R-HLT-025.

tests.name: test_send_plain_message_no_attachment_succeeds
preconditions: Patient is authenticated and session token is valid
Provider exists in the system and is assigned to the patient
No pre-existing unread messages in the thread
actions: Select the assigned provider from the recipient list
Enter a plain-text message body with no attachment
expected: HTTP 201 or UI success toast is displayed
Message appears in the sent thread with correct timestamp
Provider notification event is recorded in the audit log
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_send_plain_message_no_attachment_succeeds(authenticated_patient_page, api_client, provider_id):
    page = authenticated_patient_page
    page.goto("/messages")
    page.locator("[data-testid='recipient-select']").select_option(provider_id)
    page.locator("[data-testid='message-body']").fill("Hello, I have a question about my prescription.")
    with page.expect_response(lambda r: "/api/messages" in r.url and r.request.method == "POST") as resp_info:
        page.locator("[data-testid='send-btn']").click()
    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status == 201, f"Expected 201, got {response.status}"
    body = response.json()
    message_id = body["id"]
    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='success-toast']")).to_be_visible()
    thread_item = page.locator(f"[data-message-id='{message_id}']")
    expect(thread_item).to_be_visible()
    audit = api_client.get(f"/api/audit?event=provider_notified&message_id={message_id}")
    assert audit.status_code == 200
    assert audit.json()["count"] >= 1

tests.name: test_message_with_phi_attachment_is_blocked
preconditions: Patient is authenticated and session token is valid
Provider exists in the system and is assigned to the patient
A fixture PDF containing disallowed PHI patterns (SSN, MRN) is available at <phi_attachment_fixture>
actions: Select the assigned provider from the recipient list
Attach the PHI-containing PDF via the file input
expected: HTTP 422 or UI error banner is displayed indicating PHI detected
Message is NOT stored (no message ID returned)
No provider notification event is recorded
Audit log records a blocked PHI exfiltration attempt
trace: R-HLT-025: blocked when disallowed attachment detected
R-HLT-025: messages containing attachments shall scan for disallowed PHI exfiltration patterns
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_message_with_phi_attachment_is_blocked(authenticated_patient_page, api_client, provider_id, phi_attachment_fixture):
    page = authenticated_patient_page
    page.goto("/messages")
    page.locator("[data-testid='recipient-select']").select_option(provider_id)
    page.locator("[data-testid='message-body']").fill("Please see attached file.")
    page.locator("[data-testid='attachment-input']").set_input_files(phi_attachment_fixture)
    with page.expect_response(lambda r: "/api/messages" in r.url and r.request.method == "POST") as resp_info:
        page.locator("[data-testid='send-btn']").click()
    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status == 422, f"Expected 422, got {response.status}"
    body = response.json()
    assert "phi" in body.get("error", "").lower(), "Error should reference PHI detection"
    assert "id" not in body, "No message ID should be returned for blocked messages"
    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='error-banner']")).to_be_visible()
    audit = api_client.get("/api/audit?event=phi_blocked")
    assert audit.status_code == 200
    assert audit.json()["count"] >= 1

tests.name: test_message_with_max_size_clean_attachment_succeeds
preconditions: Patient is authenticated and session token is valid
Provider exists in the system and is assigned to the patient
A clean (no PHI) attachment fixture at exactly the maximum allowed size (e.g. 10 MB) is available at <max_size_clean_attachment_fixture>
System maximum attachment size is known and set in <MAX_ATTACHMENT_BYTES>
actions: Select the assigned provider from the recipient list
Attach the maximum-size clean file via the file input
expected: Message and attachment are stored successfully
No PHI-blocked audit entry is created for this submission
trace: R-HLT-025: messages containing attachments shall scan for disallowed PHI exfiltration patterns
tests.executable:
import os
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

MAX_ATTACHMENT_BYTES = int(os.getenv("MAX_ATTACHMENT_BYTES", str(10 * 1024 * 1024)))

def test_message_with_max_size_clean_attachment_succeeds(
    authenticated_patient_page, api_client, provider_id, max_size_clean_attachment_fixture
):
    actual_size = os.path.getsize(max_size_clean_attachment_fixture)
    assert actual_size == MAX_ATTACHMENT_BYTES, (
        f"Fixture size {actual_size} != boundary size {MAX_ATTACHMENT_BYTES}"
    )
    page = authenticated_patient_page
    page.goto("/messages")
    page.locator("[data-testid='recipient-select']").select_option(provider_id)
    page.locator("[data-testid='message-body']").fill("Boundary attachment test message.")
    page.locator("[data-testid='attachment-input']").set_input_files(max_size_clean_attachment_fixture)
    with page.expect_response(lambda r: "/api/messages" in r.url and r.request.method == "POST") as resp_info:
        page.locator("[data-testid='send-btn']").click()
    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status == 201, f"Expected 201, got {response.status}"
    message_id = response.json()["id"]
    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='success-toast']")).to_be_visible()
    audit = api_client.get(f"/api/audit?event=provider_notified&message_id={message_id}")
    assert audit.json()["count"] >= 1
    phi_audit = api_client.get(f"/api/audit?event=phi_blocked&message_id={message_id}")
    assert phi_audit.json()["count"] == 0, "Clean attachment should not trigger PHI block"

tests.name: test_message_state_transitions_draft_to_sent_to_notified
preconditions: Patient is authenticated and session token is valid
Provider exists in the system and is assigned to the patient
Message API supports observable state field: draft -> sent -> notified
actions: POST to /api/messages with status=draft to create a draft
PATCH /api/messages/{id} with status=sent to transition to sent state
Poll /api/audit until provider_notified event appears for the message
GET /api/messages/{id} and assert final state is 'notified'
expected: Draft creation returns HTTP 201 with state=draft
Send transition returns HTTP 200 with state=sent
Audit log records provider_notified within timeout
tests.executable:
import time
import pytest

POLL_TIMEOUT = 10
POLL_INTERVAL = 1

def test_message_state_transitions_draft_to_sent_to_notified(api_client, provider_id, auth_headers):
    draft_payload = {"recipient_id": provider_id, "body": "State transition test.", "status": "draft"}
    draft_resp = api_client.post("/api/messages", json=draft_payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert draft_resp.status_code == 201, f"Draft creation failed: {draft_resp.status_code}"
    draft_body = draft_resp.json()
    assert draft_body["status"] == "draft"
    message_id = draft_body["id"]
    send_resp = api_client.patch(f"/api/messages/{message_id}", json={"status": "sent"}, headers=auth_headers)
    assert send_resp.status_code == 200, f"Send transition failed: {send_resp.status_code}"
    assert send_resp.json()["status"] == "sent"
    notified = False
    deadline = time.time() + POLL_TIMEOUT
    while time.time() < deadline:
        audit = api_client.get(f"/api/audit?event=provider_notified&message_id={message_id}", headers=auth_headers)
        if audit.status_code == 200 and audit.json().get("count", 0) >= 1:
            notified = True
            break
        time.sleep(POLL_INTERVAL)
    assert notified, "Provider notification event not recorded within timeout"
    final_resp = api_client.get(f"/api/messages/{message_id}", headers=auth_headers)
    assert final_resp.status_code == 200
    assert final_resp.json()["status"] == "notified"
